name: golang-pro
description: Use when building Go applications requiring concurrent programming, microservices architecture, or high-performance systems. Invoke for goroutines, channels, Go generics, gRPC integration.
capabilities: Design and implement concurrent Go applications using goroutines, channels, and select statements
Create production-grade microservices with gRPC integration and proper error handling
Apply Go generics with type parameters and constraints for reusable code
Set up Go project structure with proper module management and internal packages
Write comprehensive table-driven tests with race detection and benchmarks
Optimize Go code performance using pprof profiling and eliminating allocations
author: jeffallan
securitySummary: Documentation skill with legitimate Go programming patterns. All 195 static findings are false positives triggered by code examples in markdown files. No malicious intent detected.
valueStatement: Building Go applications requires deep understanding of concurrency patterns, generics, and microservices architecture. This skill provides expert guidance for Go 1.21+ development with idiomatic patterns for high-performance systems.
limitations: Does not provide runtime code execution or compilation
Does not integrate with specific cloud providers or deployment platforms
Does not generate complete microservices from specifications
Requires user to apply patterns to their own codebase

useCases.title: Building Concurrent API Services
useCases.description: Design and implement high-performance Go APIs using goroutines and channels for concurrent request handling with proper context propagation.

useCases.title: Developing gRPC Microservices
useCases.description: Create type-safe microservices using Protocol Buffers and gRPC with proper interface design and error handling patterns.

useCases.title: Optimizing Go Code Performance
useCases.description: Refactor existing Go code for better performance using benchmarks, pprof analysis, and idiomatic patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Go Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a Go function that implements [feature description]. Use idiomatic Go 1.21+ patterns with proper error handling and context support.

promptTemplates.title: Concurrent Processing
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a worker pool in Go that processes items concurrently. Include graceful shutdown, proper synchronization with sync.WaitGroup, and context cancellation handling.

promptTemplates.title: Generic Utility Functions
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a generic Go function with type parameters that [generic function description]. Use appropriate constraints from the constraints package or custom interfaces.

promptTemplates.title: Complete Microservice
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a complete gRPC microservice with [service name]. Include protobuf definitions, server implementation, interface contracts, and table-driven tests with 80%+ coverage.

bestPractices: Use goroutines with clear lifecycle management and context propagation to prevent goroutine leaks
Write table-driven tests with subtests for comprehensive coverage and readable failure output
Apply the 'accept interfaces, return structs' pattern for flexible and testable code
antiPatterns: Avoid creating goroutines without channel synchronization or context cancellation handling
Do not use reflection for type assertions when type switches or generics are sufficient
Never hardcode configuration values; use functional options or environment variables instead

faq.question: What Go version does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on Go 1.21+ patterns including generics (introduced in 1.18), enhanced concurrency features, and modern tooling.

faq.question: How does this skill handle concurrency patterns?
faq.answer: It provides worker pools, channel-based synchronization, context propagation, and proper lifecycle management using sync primitives.

faq.question: Does this skill generate test code?
faq.answer: Yes, it generates table-driven tests with subtests, benchmarks, and integrates race detector flags for concurrent code.

faq.question: Can this skill help with gRPC development?
faq.answer: Yes, it provides protobuf definitions, gRPC server/client implementations, and proper error handling for microservices.

faq.question: How are generics covered in this skill?
faq.answer: The skill includes type parameters, constraints (built-in and custom), generic functions, and generic type definitions with practical examples.

faq.question: Does this skill optimize existing Go code?
faq.answer: It provides guidance on profiling with pprof, writing benchmarks, eliminating allocations, and applying idiomatic Go patterns for performance.
